# Transcrição: 01.4 AUDITING THE PROSPECT

- **Fonte:** 01.4 AUDITING THE PROSPECT
- **Duração:** 02:19
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:19]

Okay, so once you have found the prospect and you found the owner, what you're going to want to do is review the account, review what their assets are, and review what they're currently running and see where you can actually add value. So this is where you're going to want to check out their website, you're going to want to ensure that their Facebook pixel is installed correctly. So you can use the Google Chrome extension, the Facebook pixel helper to find that. And then you're going to want to check out what they're currently running for ads. So you can check that out really quick on their page, you just go on to the left hand column, and you can check out info and ads and you can see exactly what they're running. So apart from that, some of the questions that you might want to ask and you might want to ask them is what are their current issues with their user experience? Are there any current issues on their website with the user experience? Are there any things that really catch your eye that you would recommend changing? Is there a color that really doesn't look very good? Is there something that just makes them look not super credible? How is the page load speed? Are they even running Facebook ads? Does it look like they have some sort of funnel? Does it look like they're running top of funnel, middle of funnel, and bottom of funnel? Does it look like they have some sort of call to action at each one of those? Are they running the same ads or are they changing up that creative? What kind of formats are they using? Are they using videos? Are they using carousels? Are they using all the same ones? Are they using a bunch of varieties? Are they testing out different copy? Really, the list goes on and on and on. The big one and one that a lot of people don't do is testing, right? And that's something that we teach a lot is to test and test and test and constantly be testing, even when you've found a winner. So even if it's just that, even if you reach out and say, hey, I noticed you guys are only running one ad, what if you couldn't create an ad that did 10 times as good? Or what if you could test out all these different ads and some of them would perform really, really well and you could find winners and then you'd know what kind of content to create as well. So that's another thing. So that's that step in the process where you found that prospect, you found the owner and you just want to reach out and find some things that you can review on their site, on their ads or with their existing kind of ads.
